claim: No Stable, release, conformance, manual assistive-technology, package-parity, or publication claim is made by this source contract.

semantics.nativeBasis: Two native panel divs surround one focusable ARIA separator; native button siblings provide non-drag step and collapse operations.
semantics.name: Resizable.Handle requires aria-label or aria-labelledby by type and applies it to the separator.
semantics.value: aria-valuemin, aria-valuemax, aria-valuenow, localized aria-valuetext, aria-orientation, and aria-controls update with the visible size.
semantics.focusModel: The separator and each available native button are ordinary tab stops; no roving focus or focus trap is introduced.
semantics.refTargets: Root, Primary, and Secondary refs target their divs; Handle ref targets the focusable separator div.

keyboard.behavior: Moves among separator and visible step/collapse controls in DOM order.
For horizontal layouts, moves the separator spatially by step; physical direction reverses in RTL.
For vertical layouts, decreases/increases the primary block size by step.
Moves to the documented expanded min/max size.
Collapses or restores the primary panel only when Root is collapsible.

pointerTouch: The separator supports pointer capture and cancellation. Adjacent minimum 44 CSS-pixel native buttons provide decrement, increment, and optional collapse/restore operations without dragging.

state.controlled: value owns visible state; onValueChange reports each proposal and onValueCommit reports discrete or completed pointer changes.
state.uncontrolled: defaultValue initializes internal state; the same callbacks remain observable.
state.collapse: Collapsed primary content uses the native hidden state so it cannot retain focus or remain exposed offscreen; restore returns to the last expanded value.

responsive: Panels have zero intrinsic minimums and wrap content; no fixed text-bearing height is introduced. Consumers must choose a sequential alternative when both panels cannot remain useful at 320 CSS pixels.
consumerResponsibilities: Provide a localized separator name describing the panels whose boundary moves.
Keep controlled values synchronized and do not override aria value, orientation, controls, role, or tabIndex attributes.
Provide an application-specific sequential presentation when two side-by-side panels are not usable at narrow widths; use SplitPane when the built-in stack mode is required.
Localize messages and value formatting and revalidate focus, target size, contrast, zoom, and RTL after CSS overrides.
